fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Mississippi Respiratory Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a respiratory therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Mississippi - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: respiratory therapist license reciprocity mississippi, how to transfer respiratory therapist license to mississippi, respiratory therapist compact mississippi, mississippi respiratory therapist endorsement application

board.name: Mississippi DOH — Professional Licensure — RT
board.mailing_address: 1867 Crane Ridge Drive, Suite 200B, Jackson, MS 39216

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) examination
quick_facts.ce_requirement: 24 contact hours of continuing education every two years

license_types.title: Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a respiratory therapist in Mississippi.

steps.title: Create an Account and Apply Online
steps.description: Register for an account and complete the Respiratory Therapist licensure application through the Mississippi State Board of Medical Licensure's Licensure Gateway.

steps.title: Complete Background Check
steps.description: Undergo a state and federal criminal background check as required by the Board.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts from your respiratory care program and your highest earned degree to be sent directly to the Board.

steps.title: Verify NBRC Examination
steps.description: Provide verification of your National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) Registry Examination results.

steps.title: Verify Prior Licenses
steps.description: Submit verification of any prior or current respiratory therapist licenses held in other states or territories.

steps.title: Submit NBRC Card
steps.description: Upload a copy of your current NBRC card to your online application.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBRC. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Mississippi respiratory therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Mississippi have reciprocity for respiratory therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers respiratory therapist practice in Mississippi. To work in Mississippi, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my respiratory therapist license to Mississippi?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Mississippi while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Respiratory Therapist in Mississippi
hero.dek: To apply for a Respiratory Therapist license in Mississippi, you must submit your application and supporting documents through the Mississippi State Board of Medical Licensure's Licensure Gateway.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal

official_links.registration_steps: Visit the Licensure Gateway, click 'Register for an Account' to create a new profile, then log in and select 'Begin a new application' for Respiratory Therapist licensure.
